title: Tranquil Korean Malai Jjigae
description: Rich, fluffy, one-pot Jeju dish featuring malai, suitable for anju. A blend of Korean and Ayurvedic flavors to reduce tamasic energy.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of sesame oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and sliced ginger and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add Korean chili flakes and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add 2 cups of rice flour and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until lightly toasted.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened and the rice is cooked.
Stir in malai, soy sauce, Korean chili paste, and brown sugar. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the malai is heated through.
Season with salt and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve hot.
Serve with additional toasted sesame oil for added flavor, if desired.
